The Problem

Renting out laptops is risky

Right now you're probably using a notebook, a spreadsheet, and a lot of trust. That's risky when every laptop is worth hundreds of dollars.

Laptops disappear

Once it leaves your shop, you can't see it, find it, or stop someone from using it.

People stop paying

You can't do anything when a customer stops paying, except call them again and again.

You don't know who you're renting to

Anyone can walk in, sign a paper, and walk out with a laptop.

Your records get messy

Spreadsheets and notebooks are easy to lose and hard to keep up to date.

What You Get

Everything you need, in one app

Four simple tools that all work together, so nothing falls through the cracks.

Lock & Find

Lock a laptop from anywhere

If someone stops paying or won't give it back, lock their laptop with one tap.

  • Lock it in seconds
  • See it on a map
  • Wipe it clean if needed
Track Your Laptops

Know where every laptop is

See every laptop you own, who has it, and what shape it's in.

  • One list of all your laptops
  • See who has each one
  • Track damage and repairs
Check Renters

Know who you're renting to

Check a customer's ID before you hand over a laptop.

  • Scan their ID and address
  • They sign on their phone
  • Spot risky renters early
Payments & Plans

See who owes you money

See every customer's plan and payment status at a glance.

  • See which laptop is on which plan
  • Get alerts when payment is late
  • Auto-locks if they don't pay

How It Works

From handoff to renewal, in four steps

01

Check

Make sure your customer is really who they say they are.

02

Hand it over

Match the laptop to their plan, then give it to them.

03

Watch

Keep an eye on the laptop and their payments.

04

Lock or renew

If they stop paying, lock it. If all is well, renew their plan.

Who It's For

Perfect for anyone renting out laptops

Laptop rental shops

Renting laptops by the day, month, or year.

Office leasing companies

Giving laptops to other businesses on a plan.

Refurbished laptop sellers

Renting fixed-up laptops instead of selling them.

Pay-over-time sellers

Selling laptops in monthly payments, not all at once.

Why Not Just Use...

Other apps only do half the job

A lock app can't see your payments. A spreadsheet can't lock a laptop. LaptopFirst does everything together.

What you need Lock apps Spreadsheets LaptopFirst
Lock a laptop from far away
Track all your laptops
Check who you're renting to
See who owes you money Sort of

Keeping Things Safe

Your customers' information stays safe

We know you're trusting us with private information. Here's how we look after it.

All information is locked up and scrambled, so no one else can read it.

Only the right people on your team can see private customer details.

We keep a record of every lock, unlock, and ID check, so you always know what happened.

Old customer files don't stick around forever. You control how long we keep them.

FAQ

Common questions from rental businesses

What is MDM? Do I really need it?

MDM stands for Mobile Device Management. It just means you can lock, find, or wipe a laptop from far away, using your phone or computer. If you rent out laptops, this is how you keep them safe.

Can I lock a laptop if someone stops paying?

Yes. If a customer misses a payment, you can lock their laptop with one click. They'll see a message telling them to pay you. Once they pay, it unlocks by itself.

How do I check if a customer is real before renting to them?

Before you hand over a laptop, the customer uploads their ID and proof of address. We check that it's real. They also sign your rental agreement right on their phone, so you're not guessing who you're dealing with.

What if a customer doesn't give the laptop back?

You can see the last place the laptop connected to the internet, and lock it right away. This makes it much easier to get your laptop back without going to court.

Does this work for both Windows laptops and MacBooks?

Yes. Whether your laptops are Windows or Mac, you manage all of them from the same simple screen.

How is this different from other apps?

Most apps only do one job: one locks laptops, another tracks who has them, another handles payments. LaptopFirst does all three together, so when someone stops paying, their laptop locks automatically — no extra work for you.

Ready to keep every laptop safe?
